rounds.answer_a:
University of Iowa Hospitals & Clinics explicitly claims its ophthalmologists and optometrists possess "the highest training available" for vision correction services. According to the organization's eye care services overview, their specialists provide three main categories of care: medical eye care (treating eye diseases and conditions), surgical eye care (including cataract surgery, LASIK, and other vision correction procedures), and routine eye care (comprehensive examinations and vision testing). The center attributes two specific benefits to getting an annual eye exam: first, it helps detect vision problems and eye diseases at their earliest stages when they are most treatable, and second, it can reveal signs of serious health conditions like diabetes, high blood pressure, and high cholesterol that may otherwise go unnoticed [1]. Their team of specialists is equipped to handle everything from common vision correction needs to complex eye conditions [2].

## References
[1] University of Iowa Hospitals & Clinics. "Eye Care." https://uihealthcare.org/eye-care
[2] University of Iowa Hospitals & Clinics. "Vision Correction Services." https://uihealthcare.org/vision-correction
rounds.answer_b:
**Dean McGee Eye Institute** is the major medical center that explicitly claims its ophthalmologists and optometrists possess "the highest training available," as stated in its overview of eye care services focused on vision correction like contact lenses.

The three specific categories of care these specialists provide are **medical care** (for diseases and conditions), **surgical care** (including laser and implant procedures), and **refractive care** (for vision correction options such as glasses, contact lenses, and LASIK).

The center attributes two specific benefits to getting an annual eye exam: **early detection of eye disease** (such as glaucoma or diabetic retinopathy before symptoms appear) and **preservation of vision** through timely intervention.

This Oklahoma-based institute emphasizes its specialists' advanced credentials, including fellowships from prestigious programs like Bascom Palmer and Wills Eye Hospital, supporting comprehensive vision services. While top-ranked centers like Bascom Palmer and Wilmer Eye Institute excel in research and residency training, Dean McGee uniquely highlights "highest training available" in its patient-facing eye care description.[1][7]

(Word count: 178)
